overview: Over eight years in Peru, ministry unfolded through deep local partnerships — planting seeds of faith, walking alongside pastors, and responding to real needs in both city and village contexts.

blocks.title: Community Outreach
blocks.description: Regular visits to neighborhoods and highland villages brought food distributions, medical referral support, and open conversations about faith. Outreach teams included local believers to ensure lasting connection.
blocks.imageAlt: Volunteers serving food to community members

blocks.title: Church Planting
blocks.description: Working with Peruvian pastors, Daniel helped establish and strengthen house churches and small congregations. Training focused on biblical preaching, prayer, and sustainable local leadership.

blocks.title: Teaching & Discipleship
blocks.description: Weekly Bible studies, one-on-one mentoring, and leadership workshops equipped believers to grow in faith and serve their communities. Materials were developed in Spanish with cultural sensitivity.

blocks.title: Humanitarian Work
blocks.description: Practical aid addressed urgent needs — clean water projects, school supplies, and emergency relief after seasonal flooding. Every act of service opened doors for gospel-centered relationships.

blocks.title: Youth Programs
blocks.description: Sports ministries, music workshops, and after-school tutoring gave young people a safe community. Many youth came to faith and now lead peer Bible studies in their neighborhoods.
blocks.imageAlt: Youth gathered in a classroom setting

blocks.title: Local Partnerships
blocks.description: Collaboration with Peruvian churches, NGOs, and community leaders ensured ministry was culturally grounded and locally owned. Partnership remains the foundation of ongoing impact.
blocks.imageAlt: Group of people joining hands in unity
